TIM 172 A, MOT-I, Lecture #2, 9/30/25

   Agenda :

1. Quick summary of  L#1

2.  HW #1 (due on Thursday, 10/2/25)

3.  TIM 172 P
       • placed all students in group
       • posted the Project Proposal assignment
       • for the whole TIM 172A, the "FILES"
         tab on Canvas will contain a "TIM 172P" folder

TIM 172A  IMPORTANT NOTE :

  • All submitted work must be TYPEWRITTEN
  • We will NOT accept handwritten work
  • Figures, graphics can be hand-drawn, &
    inserted in the typewritten text.
1.   Quick  summary  of  L # 1
        - Fast  cycle  competitors - FAANG & MANGA -
        can  balance  the  conflicting  trade-offs
        between product quality, speed, & cost ;
        and  can  successfully manage  risk.
        -  This  course  will  develop the building blocks,
           & a  framework - MDC - for integrating
           these  blocks
        -  Professionals (Engineering, Business, ....)
           generally  use  a  5-step  structured
           problem-solving process (SPSP):

      Define ⟶ Plan (or Process or Approach) ⟶ Execute ⌐
                  Learn/Generalize ⟵ Checking ⟵
2.    HW # 1,  Prob. # 1

Pre-liminary instructions ( for all the work
   - homeworks, exams, project tasks - done in
   TIM 172A/P  and beyond.) :

  1. Develop a planning (habit)
            - Create a simple plan (what, when,
              how long, .....) for doing all the
              problems in an assigment

  2. Develop a structured problem -solving (habit.)
            - Download the SPSP handout on Canvas
            - For every problem, "copy-and-paste"
              the handout into your proposed solution
            - Decide which sub- steps ( for each
              of the 5 primary steps) are relevant
              or necessary to the solution of your
              particular problem
                 ⇒ Customize the SPSP for
                    your particular problem.

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Lecture #2 agenda for TIM 172A covering L#1 recap, HW #1, TIM 172P logistics, and a note requiring typewritten submissions.
extracted_json.summary_gist: This page lists the agenda for TIM 172A (MOT-I) Lecture #2 on 9/30/25: a quick summary of Lecture 1, HW #1 due Thursday 10/2/25, and TIM 172P updates (students placed in groups, Project Proposal assignment posted, and a "TIM 172P" folder added under the "FILES" tab on Canvas). An important note states all submitted work must be typewritten, handwritten work will not be accepted, but figures/graphics may be hand-drawn and inserted into the typewritten text. A recap of Lecture 1: fast-cycle competitors (FAANG & MANGA) balance trade-offs among product quality, speed, and cost while managing risk. The course develops building blocks and an integrating framework (MDC). It introduces the 5-step structured problem-solving process (SPSP): Define → Plan → Execute → Checking → Learn/Generalize. Two preliminary habits to develop for all course work: (1) a planning habit—create a simple plan (what, when, how long) for doing all problems in an assignment; and (2) a structured problem-solving habit—download the SPSP handout from Canvas, copy-and-paste it into each proposed solution, and decide which sub-steps of the 5 primary steps are relevant, customizing the SPSP for each particular problem. The page works HW #1, Prob #1, where the given problem ("Improve the existing cell-phone") is identified as vague and open-ended. Step 1 of the SPSP is to define the real problem, which requires reflection and questioning, illustrated with a rough conceptual sketch (Customer Needs → Assess → Improve). The problem is then broken into three sub-problems: SP1 establishes generic customer needs, SP2 assesses how well existing products satisfy them, and SP3 identifies ways to improve cell-phones with respect to SP1's needs and SP2's assessment. Notes outline Step 2 (Create a Plan) of a problem-solving framework applied to cell-phone design. Part (a) lists assumptions: identifying who you are (an engineer or marketing analyst at a company like Apple or Samsung), who the solution is for (new product development team, CTO, CEO), and which type/manufacturer of cell-phone to focus on (be practical). Part (b) identifies needed information: how cell-phones work (functions, customer needs, and how they are realized as FORM, referencing HowStuffWorks.com) and other unspecified information. The page outlines a plan for tackling three design sub-problems. SP1 (Establish needs) is addressed by understanding how cell-phones work, internet research, and compiling a list of customer needs (functions, features). SP2 (Assess how well) uses own experience, internet consumer research, and customer surveys. SP3 (Improve) relies on internet research and creative brainstorming. These notes cover Step 3 (Execute the plan) of the SPSP process. The plan is executed across sub-plans SP1, SP2, and SP3, each with estimated time durations (1-2 hrs, 2-4 hrs, 2-4 hrs). The notes then ask how to present results, suggesting a table format with a Needs column (examples: ease of use, performance, reliability), an Assessment column, and additional unspecified columns. Notes covering the final steps of the SPSP method (Step 4: check your work/reflect; Step 5: learn & generalize about cell-phones and yourself as a problem-solver), a note that HW #1 Prob 2 is a simpler version of Prob 1, and the TA structure for TIM 172A split into Infrastructure (Canvas) handled by Vihan and Customer & Grader facing handled by Fariha. Reminder: do not use Canvas Notifications to contact the staff.
